claim: Sentry provides error monitoring with actionable insights to resolve code-related issues across any language or framework.
Sentry supports SDKs for Android, .NET, Go, Swift, JavaScript, Python, React, Ruby, Next.js, PHP, Laravel, ASP.NET Core, Spring Boot, Vue, Angular, Solid, Svelte, Astro.
Sentry’s tooling helps Disney+ maintain high-quality service for its tens of millions of global subscribers.
Roni Avidov, R&D Team Lead at Monday.com, states that Sentry saved an insane amount of developer time across 150+ developers.
Sentry offers integrations with GitHub, Slack, Sentry CLI, and Sentry MCP.
Sentry provides features such as stack traces with local variables, breadcrumbs, frequency monitoring, and user impact analysis.
Sentry offers AI debugging tools including Seer, Agent Autofix, and AI Code Review.
Sentry provides a free trial and demo options for prospective users.
Sentry is used by Disney+ to maintain high-quality service for tens of millions of global subscribers.
Instacart uses Sentry as a reliable indicator of software issues across its services.
Sentry claims to be considered "not bad" by millions of developers.
Sentry advertises that integration requires only five lines of code.
Sentry offers AI debugging features called Seer and an AI code review agent.
Seer is available to all Sentry users on a paid plan (Team, Business, or Enterprise) at $40 per active contributor per month.
An active contributor is defined as anyone who commits two or more pull requests in a connected repository.
Seer supports all programming languages and frameworks supported by Sentry, including web (frontend and backend), mobile, and desktop.
Seer is enabled for all Sentry users on a paid plan or trial; users are prompted to enable Seer and generative AI features within the Sentry interface the first time they attempt to use them.

Seer respects user privacy: AI‑generated output is shown only to the user, data is not used to train generative AI models without explicit consent, and trusted subprocessors cannot use the data to train their own models.
Sentry provides a Logs product that connects structured logs to errors, traces, and code in a single view.
Logs in Sentry are automatically trace‑connected, allowing users to view logs inline with spans using span_id, trace_id, or custom fields.
Sentry’s structured logging lets users attach arbitrary properties (e.g., customer_id, payment_id) to log messages and filter/search by those fields.
Sentry supports log‑based alerts that can trigger on patterns, anomalies, or spikes in structured values.
Sentry offers log dashboards for visualizing key trends and fields from structured logs.
Sentry provides SDK examples for enabling logs in Python, PHP, and Java (Android) with a simple initialization snippet.
Sentry’s documentation states that the Logs product works with every technology except those explicitly unsupported.
Seer Autofix reads the stack trace, traces the root cause through your codebase, and drafts a fix before you finish reading the alert.
Seer Autofix can automatically create pull requests with code fixes for issues it is confident it can fix.
Seer Autofix integrates with coding agents Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and Claude to hand off fixes with full context.
Seer Autofix supports every language and framework that Sentry can instrument, including web, mobile, and desktop.
Komal Ali, Director of Engineering at Pulumi, is quoted on the page praising Seer Autofix's impact on bug load.
Seer Agent is included with a Seer subscription.
Seer is available on paid plans (Team, Business, or Enterprise) at $40 per active contributor per month.
An active contributor is defined as anyone who commits two or more pull requests in a connected repository.
Only the user and their team members can see what Seer Agent generates; data is never shown to other Sentry customers.
Seer Agent can investigate across multiple services using Sentry's distributed tracing data.
Seer Agent can be used both during an active incident and for post‑mortems, but is particularly useful during an active incident.
Seer Agent compresses incident investigation into a conversational interface that surfaces relevant errors, traces the blast radius, and connects signals to a cause.
getsentry is a GitHub organization.
The primary languages used by the getsentry organization are Python, Shell, and TypeScript.
The getsentry/sentry repository has 44,186 stars, 4,728 forks, is written in Python, has 2,106 open issues, and its last push was on 2026-06-29T11:15:20Z.
The getsentry/self-hosted repository has 9,430 stars, 1,958 forks, is written in Shell, has 134 open issues, and its last push was on 2026-06-29T09:32:42Z.
The getsentry/sentry-javascript repository has 8,699 stars, 1,787 forks, is written in TypeScript, has 639 open issues, and its last push was on 2026-06-29T10:26:46Z.
The getsentry/XcodeBuildMCP repository has 5,997 stars, 298 forks, is written in TypeScript, has 20 open issues, and its last push was on 2026-06-29T07:45:38Z.
The getsentry/responses repository has 4,349 stars, 369 forks, is written in Python, has 36 open issues, and its last push was on 2026-06-26T14:28:47Z.
The getsentry/sentry-python repository has 2,196 stars, 630 forks, is written in Python, has 389 open issues, and its last push was on 2026-06-29T11:13:44Z.

verdict.bull: Strong traction with enterprise users, broad language/framework support, and differentiated AI debugging features.
verdict.bear: Critical gaps in market and team information make it difficult to assess scalability and competitive durability.

risk_matrix.reason: Product description is clear and includes privacy safeguards; no obvious risks from the provided facts.
Broad SDK and integration support, AI tools, and low-code setup indicate a mature engineering foundation.
Only a single fact about contributor definition is available, leaving team capacity and execution risk unassessed.
Virtually no market data is provided beyond a free trial mention, making competitive positioning and TAM unknown.
Strong customer logos, developer testimonials, and high GitHub engagement suggest low traction risk.

section_summaries.overview: Sentry offers error monitoring with AI-powered debugging tools (Seer) and a logs product, priced at $40 per active contributor per month on paid plans.
section_summaries.engineering: The platform supports a wide range of SDKs and integrations, includes AI features like Seer Agent and Autofix, and provides trace-connected structured logging with low-code setup.
section_summaries.hiring: Only a definition of an active contributor is provided, with no details on team size, hiring needs, or organizational structure.
section_summaries.market: The only market-related fact is that Sentry offers a free trial and demo; no data on market size, competition, or growth is available.
section_summaries.traction: Sentry demonstrates strong traction with high-profile users like Disney+ and Monday.com, and its main GitHub repositories have tens of thousands of stars and active development.
